steps.title: Use the triangle angle sum
steps.content: The angles in a triangle sum to $180^{\circ}$. Add the two given angles, then subtract their sum from $180^{\circ}$ to find angle $A$.

steps.title: Apply the triangle angle sum property
steps.content:
The sum of all angles in any triangle equals $180^{\circ}$. Set up the equation:
$$A + B + C = 180^{\circ}$$
Substitute the given values $B = 55^{\circ}$ and $C = 18^{\circ}$:
$$A + 55^{\circ} + 18^{\circ} = 180^{\circ}$$

steps.title: Solve for angle A
steps.content:
Combine the known angles on the left side:
$$A + 73^{\circ} = 180^{\circ}$$
Subtract $73^{\circ}$ from both sides:
$$A = 180^{\circ} - 73^{\circ} = 107^{\circ}$$
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
